title: centaurea tenuifolia subsp. mariolensis
intro: Centaurea tenuifolia subsp. mariolensis, also known as a species of the genus Centaurea, is a plant commonly referred to as the mountain knapweed or the narrow-leaved knapweed. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Centaurea tenuifolia subsp. mariolensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
